--- SOURCE 1: Observation of electron-antineutrino disappearance at Daya Bay ---
URL: https://inspirehep.net/literature/1093266

SUMMARY:
The Daya Bay Reactor Neutrino Experiment has measured a non-zero value for the neutrino mixing angle θ 13 with a significance of 5.2 standard deviations.

--- SOURCE 2: Observation of electron-antineutrino disappearance at Daya Bay ---
URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/1203.1669

SUMMARY:
The Daya Bay Reactor Neutrino Experiment has measured a non-zero value for the neutrino mixing angle \theta_{13} with a significance of 5.2 standard deviations.

--- SOURCE 4: Electron-antineutrino Disappearance Observed by the Daya Bay ... ---
URL: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0090375214000398

SUMMARY:
With a 116.8 kton-GWth-day live-time exposure in 139 days, 28,909 and 205,308 electron-antineutrino candidates were detected at the far hall and near halls

enriched_points.expanded_content: Daya Bay achieved a significant measurement of electron antineutrino disappearance, detecting 28,909 candidates, confirming that neutrinos oscillate between flavors.
The experiment successfully extracted the mixing angle θ₁₃ with a precision of 5.2 standard deviations, providing crucial data for understanding neutrino mixing.
By observing the disappearance of electron antineutrinos, Daya Bay provided critical experimental confirmation of the neutrino oscillation theory, enhancing our comprehension of particle physics.
Daya Bay’s findings contribute to the broader understanding of neutrino masses, shaping future research on the nature of these elusive particles.
